headline: Week two: target cleared again, calf stays quiet
narrative: Second week of the build and the pattern from week one holds: the vert came in comfortably over target, and time on feet edged past the ask too. The focus was extending hike duration, and the triple-rep morning on Alt del Governador did the heavy lifting there, backed by a proper long outing over Mill Hill and Kinder Downfall — the first Peaks day of the block and the longest on-foot session yet. The engine work kept ticking with two solid rides and a swim, plus a strength session in the gym.

On the calf: nothing to report, which after last week's twinge at run club is the news we wanted. Alderley parkrun was sensibly taken as a run/walk — keep treating flat running as a probe, not a session. Next week asks for the longest hike yet plus downhill volume, with the target stepping up again. Two weeks of small surpluses is a good habit; the job now is to stretch duration without chasing the number.
